mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when RSAFS is expanding.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and suppresses risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that boosts wage and consumption expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days that neither proxy achieves independently.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive during joint PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease periods across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when monetary policy is accommodative; when the federal funds rate is elevated, the supply signal is overwhelmed by discount rate effects and loses predictive power.
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with low real rates
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is below 3% and INDPRO is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales momentum captures near-term consumer demand impulses that generate immediate risk-on positioning and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when RSAFS accelerates month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate elevation above 5% triggers earnings recession fears and cyclical equity de-rating, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surprises predict multi-month equity trends through revisions in permanent income and corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with contained consumer price inflation jointly signal a Goldilocks demand environment that boosts discretionary and housing-sensitive equities, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns that neither series delivers alone.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during periods of jointly rising HOUST and contained CPIAUCSL inflation.
